title: Control and Monitor the Use of Mobile Code
description: Control and monitor the use of mobile code.

checks.name: PowerShell Execution Policy is RemoteSigned or AllSigned
checks.purpose: Verify PowerShell execution policy is RemoteSigned or AllSigned to control execution of downloaded and unsigned scripts (a form of mobile code)

checks.name: Windows Script Host is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Script Host Enabled = 0 in registry to prevent execution of VBScript and JScript mobile code outside of controlled environments

checks.name: AppLocker or WDAC Policies are Enforced
checks.purpose: Confirm AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control policies are active and in Enforce mode to control which scripts and executables may run

checks.name: PowerShell Execution Policy is RemoteSigned or AllSigned
checks.purpose: Verify PowerShell execution policy restricts unsigned script execution on the server

checks.name: Windows Script Host is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Script Host is disabled on the server to prevent VBScript/JScript mobile code execution

checks.name: AppLocker or WDAC Policies are Enforced
checks.purpose: Confirm application control policies are active on the server to restrict unauthorized code execution

checks.name: Mobile Code Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify a documented policy identifies authorized mobile code technologies and controls their use â€” policy review only

checks.name: noexec Option on /tmp and /home
checks.purpose: Verify /tmp and /home are mounted with the noexec option to prevent execution of downloaded scripts and mobile code in user-writable directories

checks.name: No World-Writable Directories in PATH
checks.purpose: Verify no world-writable directories exist in the system PATH, preventing injection of malicious executables that could masquerade as system commands

checks.name: Mobile Code Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify organizational policy controls authorized mobile code â€” policy review only

checks.name: noexec Option on /tmp and /home
checks.purpose: Verify /tmp and /home are mounted with the noexec option to prevent execution of downloaded scripts and mobile code in user-writable directories

checks.name: No World-Writable Directories in PATH
checks.purpose: Verify no world-writable directories exist in the system PATH, preventing injection of malicious executables that could masquerade as system commands

checks.name: Mobile Code Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify organizational policy controls authorized mobile code â€” policy review only

remediation: Set PowerShell execution policy to RemoteSigned or AllSigned via GPO. Disable Windows Script Host via registry (HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Script Host\Settings Enabled = 0). Deploy AppLocker or WDAC policies in Enforce mode. On Linux, add noexec to /tmp and /home mount options in /etc/fstab and remove world-writable directories from PATH.
